fix_applied.action: Replaced fake stability calculation with REAL stability using hit_history

stability_values_after_real_fix.note: Stability now truly varies (was constant 0.5 before)

comparison_across_fixes.initial_state_collapse: HOT=375, COLD=0, CHAOTIC=0, TRANSITION=206 (HOT 62.5% - state collapse)
comparison_across_fixes.after_threshold_fix_only: HOT=73, COLD=13, CHAOTIC=35, TRANSITION=460 (TRANSITION 76.67% - still biased)
comparison_across_fixes.after_fake_stability_fix: HOT=18, COLD=13, CHAOTIC=14, TRANSITION=236 (TRANSITION 78.67% - still biased)
comparison_across_fixes.after_real_stability_fix: HOT=18, COLD=21, CHAOTIC=28, TRANSITION=214 (TRANSITION 71.33% - all states present)

remaining_issue.root_cause: StateDetector stability threshold (stability < 0.6 → TRANSITION vote) fires frequently because stability ranges 0.11-0.70 with mean 0.41, meaning ~70% of values are below 0.6
remaining_issue.note: This is a StateDetector voting structure issue, not a stability calculation issue. All 4 required states ARE present - state space is no longer collapsed.

achievement.note: Previously HOT dominated at 62.5% with some states at 0. Now all states present with TRANSITION dominating (structural bias, not collapse)

stability_fix_retry_report.gate_result: PARTIAL FAIL - TRANSITION dominates (71.33%) but all 4 required states present and stability varies
